description_long: This object is a hand axe featuring a robust, slightly curved wooden handle with a visible wood grain texture. The handle is ergonomically shaped for a comfortable grip and is attached to a large, black metal head. The axe head has a broad, flat blade with a sharp, straight cutting edge and a thick poll on the opposite side, suitable for splitting or chopping tasks. The blade is solid and unadorned, with a matte finish that reduces glare. The mounting between the handle and the head appears traditional, with the handle wedged securely into the eye of the blade. No visible markings, engravings, or labels are present on either the handle or the blade.
description: A hand axe with a curved wooden handle and a broad, black metal blade, designed for chopping or splitting tasks.
description_view_0: The axe is seen from a three-quarter angle, highlighting the curved wooden handle and the full profile of the black blade.
description_view_1: A side view showing the length and curvature of the handle and the full width of the blade's cutting edge.
description_view_2: A rear three-quarter view displaying the back of the axe head and the handle's attachment point.
description_view_3: A direct side view focusing on the thickness of the blade and the handle's ergonomic curve.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with visible grain, ergonomic grip, oval cross-section, and a traditional style.
attribute_blade_design: Broad, single-edged blade with a straight cutting edge, thick poll, and no decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the handle inserted through the eye of the blade.
